name: Shambhala
description: A towering hypercoaster built for huge airtime moments. Smooth, fast, and often considered a must-do for coaster fans — ride it early for shorter queues.

name: Dragon Khan
description: A legendary coaster known for its intense inversions and iconic roar. A classic that still feels powerful today — try a front-row ride for maximum speed feeling.

name: Furius Baco
description: Explosive launch coaster with a punchy acceleration. Fast, low-to-the-ground and wild through twists — a great ‘short but intense’ adrenaline hit.

name: Red Force
description: Extreme acceleration to incredible speed and height. A pure adrenaline tower launch — perfect if you want the biggest ‘wow’ moment in the resort.

name: Hurakan Condor
description: Free-fall drop tower with breathtaking views before the plunge. The anticipation is half the fun — not for the faint-hearted.

name: Grand Canyon Rapids
description: Raft-style water ride with spins and splashes. Great mid-day to cool down — you will get wet.

name: Tutuki Splash
description: Big splash boat ride with a dramatic final drop. Expect a soaked finale — stash valuables or use a poncho.

name: Silver River Flume
description: Classic log flume adventure with scenic moments and a satisfying drop. A timeless water ride that’s fun for most ages.

name: Sea Odyssey
description: Immersive simulator journey — a good break from heat and queues. Great for families and anyone wanting something chill.

name: El Diablo – Tren de la Mina
description: Family mine train coaster through themed scenery. A perfect ‘bridge’ ride before the big coasters — re-rideable and fun.

name: Tomahawk
description: Compact family coaster — simple, fun, and easy to repeat. Great warm-up if you’re building confidence.

name: Tami-Tami
description: Gentle family coaster — a perfect first coaster and a nice ‘reset’ ride between bigger thrills.
